text: Southwest Airlines stock is falling after the airline posts a wider-than-expected loss in the fourth quarter.

title: Southwest Airlines stock falls after much wider-than-expected loss, revenue miss and warning of more losses
text: Shares of Southwest Airlines Co. LUV shed 2.4% in premarket trading Thursday, after the air carrier reported a much wider-than-expected fourth-quarter loss, and warned of another loss in the current quarter, while apologizing for the “operational disruptions” during the holiday travel season. The company swung to a net loss of $220 million, or 37 cents a share, from net income of $68 million, or 11 cents a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Southwest posts $220 million loss last quarter after holiday meltdown
text: Southwest's holiday meltdown cost it hundreds of millions of dollars.

title: SOUTHWEST AIRLINES REPORTS FOURTH QUARTER AND FULL YEAR 2022 RESULTS
text: DALLAS , Jan. 26, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Southwest Airlines Co. (NYSE: LUV) (the "Company") today reported its fourth quarter and full year 2022 financial results: Fourth quarter net loss of $220 million, or $0.37 loss per diluted share Fourth quarter net loss, excluding special items1, of $226 million, or $0.38 loss per diluted share Full year net income of $539 million, or $0.87 per diluted share Full year net income, excluding special items, of $723 million, or $1.16 per diluted share Record fourth quarter and full year operating revenues of $6.2 billion and $23.8 billion, respectively Liquidity2 of $13.3 billion, well in excess of debt outstanding of $8.1 billion Bob Jordan, President and Chief Executive Officer, stated, "Due to the operational disruptions in late December, which resulted in more than 16,700 flight cancellations, we incurred a fourth quarter pre-tax negative impact of approximately $800 million (or approximately $620 million on an after-tax basis), which resulted in a fourth quarter 2022 net loss. Despite the negative financial impacts in first quarter 2022 due to the Omicron variant and in fourth quarter 2022 due to the operational disruptions, we generated full year 2022 net income, excluding special items, of $723 million.

title: Airline Stocks Slump After FAA Grounds Domestic Flights
text: Airline stocks weren't doing so hot in pre-market trading Wednesday after the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) grounded domestic flights. The reason behind this is a computer outage affecting the Notice to Air Missions (NOTAM) system.

text: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has ordered airlines to pause all domestic departures until 9 a.m. Eastern Time.

title: All U.S. domestic flights grounded as key FAA system goes down
text: Flights across the U.S. – including potentially all that fly through any domestic U.S. airspace – are delayed this morning as a major system outage means the Federal Aviation Authority (FAA) can't send out key hazard notices to commercial pilots.

title: Airline stocks tumble as FAA computer outage grounds flights
text: Airline stocks fell premarket Wednesday after a Federal Aviation Administration computer outage grounded U.S. flights.

title: Airline stocks fell premarket after FAA says all U.S. flights grounded over computer outage
text: Airlines stocks fell across the board in premarket trade Wednesday, after the Federal Aviation Administration said a computer outage had led to all U.S. fights being grounded. The agency said on its website that its “Notice to Air Missions” system has been activated “to address the equipment outage issues for the U.S. NOTAM system.

title: Southwest Airlines promotes executives following operational meltdown
text: Southwest Airlines announced promotions for five executives on Tuesday, days after saying its operational disaster over the holidays would result in a fourth-quarter loss

title: 4 Important Leadership Lessons From Southwest Airlines' Holiday Meltdown
text: Last month, Southwest had a terrible, horrible, no good, very bad week — and every business can learn something from it.

title: SOUTHWEST AIRLINES LAUNCHES SALE WITH FARES AS LOW AS $49 ONE-WAY
text: Carrier offers fares as low as $49 one-way for travel through May 17, 2023 DALLAS , Jan. 10, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Southwest Airlines Co.  (NYSE: LUV) today launches its seasonal fare sale through Jan. 12, 2023, offering fares as low as $49 one-way for travel taking place Jan. 31, 2023, through May 17, 2023. Visit Southwest.com  to view fares as well as terms and conditions.

title: Southwest Airlines promotes network planning executive to broader role
text: Southwest Airlines Co. LUV, +2.03% late Monday announced a slew of promotions and re-arrangements, including the promotion of its vice president of network planning, Adam Decaire, to senior VP of network planning and network operations control. Decaire will continue to report to Chief Operating Officer Andrew Watterson.

title: SOUTHWEST AIRLINES ANNOUNCES LEADERSHIP PROMOTIONS
text: DALLAS , Jan. 9, 2023 /PRNewswire/ --  Southwest Airlines Co. (NYSE: LUV) today announced five Leadership promotions across several Departments. These changes, which are effective immediately, represent phase two of the organizational structure work that began in September 2022.

title: Analysts Pick 9 'Cheap' Favorites As Value Stocks Still Dominate
text: Value stocks are ruling the S&P 500 again following their standout run last year. And analysts are picking their favorites for the year.

title: Southwest (LUV) May Incur Q4 Loss Post Flight Cancellations
text: Southwest (LUV) expects capacity in fourth-quarter 2022 to decline 6% versus fourth-quarter 2019 actuals.

title: Southwest's strong 2022 rebound soured by holiday debacle
text: Southwest Airlines is anticipating a money-losing fourth quarter after a winter storm and technology meltdown led to nearly 17,000 canceled flights and stranded hundreds of thousands of holiday travelers.

title: Southwest Airlines: The Big Winter Blow
text: Southwest Airlines announced hundreds of millions in costs due to the winter storm. Preliminary cost estimate matches my model outcome and is a considerable windfall.

title: Southwest Airlines says holiday cancellations could amount to $825M loss
text: Southwest Airlines expects to rack up a significant loss, as much as $825 million, from the operational meltdown that led to nearly 17,000 flight cancellations.

title: Southwest's holiday travel meltdown to cost airline nearly $1B
text: It took Southwest eight days to recover just before the New Year's Day weekend, while other major airlines were up and running quickly after the storm passed.

title: Southwest Airlines to book a 4Q loss after hitting major air pocket in December
text: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) Company expects to book a fourth-quarter loss after the beleaguered airlines experienced a complete operational meltdown in December, which led to widespread flight cancellations.  The Dallas-headquartered company canceled over 16,700 flights in the final 10 days of 2022 as a winter storm threw its operations completely out of gear during the crucial holiday-travel period.

title: Southwest estimates its December meltdown will cost it up to $825 million in lost revenue, passenger reimbursements, and other costs
text: The airline said Friday it canceled more than 16,700 flights in late December as a bitter winter storm wrought havoc on its operations.

title: Southwest Reveals Impact of Flight Chaos on Earnings. The Stock Is Falling.
text: The carrier estimated a hit of $725 million to $825 million from the disruption, which saw it cancel more than 16,000 flights between Dec. 21 and Jan. 2.
